dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4285 IS: Guard Equal Benefits for Federal Missions Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Blackburn introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To ensure National Guard members performing full-time National Guard duty in support of Federal law enforcement operations receive benefits equivalent to those provided for service during a national emergency.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Guard Equal Benefits for Federal Missions Act.

section.header: Treatment of certain title 32 service as national emergency service

subsection.text: Chapter 5 of title 32, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

section.header: Treatment of certain full-time National Guard duty for benefit eligibility

subsection.text: For purposes of any provision of law that provides benefits based on service in response to a national emergency, full-time National Guard duty performed under section 502(f) shall be treated as service in response to a national emergency if such duty—

paragraph.text: is authorized by the President or the Secretary of Defense; and
is performed in direct support of Federal law enforcement operations, including operations conducted in coordination with—

subparagraph.text: the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives; or
any other Federal law enforcement agency designated by the Secretary of Defense; and

paragraph.text: is carried out for the purpose of addressing—

subparagraph.text: other threats to public safety as determined by the Secretary of Defense.

subsection.text: Service described in subsection (a) shall be deemed qualifying service for purposes of—

paragraph.text: section 1145 of title 10 (Transitional Assistance Management Program);
chapter 33 of title 38 (Post-9/11 GI Bill); and
any other Federal benefit requiring service in response to a national emergency.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the authority of the President to declare a national emergency.
The table of sections at the beginning of chapter 5 of title 32, United States Code, is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 510 the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: 511. Treatment of certain full-time National Guard duty for benefit eligibility.
